metadata.name: Discover Inputs
metadata.description: Smart file discovery protocol with multiple loading strategies. Resolves file references, expands path patterns, and loads content into the workflow context for downstream step consumption.

triggers.trigger: Before each workflow step that declares `inputs`
When a step references files via `ref:` prefix
When a dependency-resolve plan lists required assets

strategy.description: Read the entire file contents into the workflow context as a single block. The file is stored under its resolved key in the context map.

constraints.supported_formats: json, yaml, yml, md, txt, xml

procedure.step: Resolve the file path (see path-resolution below).
Verify the file exists on disk.
Check file size against max_file_size constraint.
If file exceeds max_file_size, automatically escalate to SELECTIVE_LOAD strategy and log a warning.
Read the entire file content.
Parse the content according to its format (JSON, YAML, etc.).
Store the parsed content in the workflow context under the key derived from the input declaration (e.g., `context.inputs.{input_name}`).

strategy.description: Load only the specified sections, keys, or line ranges from a file. Reduces context consumption for large files where only a subset of the data is relevant to the current step.

procedure.step: Resolve the file path.
Verify the file exists on disk.
Parse the selector expression from the input declaration.

Read the file and extract only the matching sections: - For json_path: evaluate the JSONPath expression against parsed JSON. - For yaml_key: traverse the YAML structure to the specified key. - For line_range: read only the specified line numbers. - For heading: extract the markdown section under the given heading (up to the next heading of equal or higher level).
Store the extracted content in the workflow context. Include metadata about which sections were loaded for traceability.

strategy.description: First read a directory index or manifest file, then selectively load individual files based on what the index reveals. This is a two-phase discovery process for directory-structured assets.

constraints.requires: directory path with an index file

procedure.step: Resolve the directory path.
Verify the directory exists on disk.
Locate the index file by checking index_file_names in order. Use the first one found. If none found, fall back to a directory listing and construct a virtual index from filenames.
Parse the index file to get the catalogue of available assets.
Match the step's input requirements against the index entries. Determine which specific files need to be loaded.
Load each matched file using FULL_LOAD or SELECTIVE_LOAD depending on the file size and the step's selector requirements.
Store all loaded assets in the workflow context as a collection, preserving the index structure for downstream reference.

path_resolution.description: All file paths in workflow inputs go through path resolution before any loading strategy is applied. Resolution handles three cases: ref-prefixed paths, pattern-based paths, and literal paths.

resolver.description: Paths prefixed with `ref:` are project-relative references. Strip the `ref:` prefix and prepend the project root path.

procedure.step: Detect the `ref:` prefix in the path string.
Strip the prefix, leaving the relative path portion.
Resolve `{project-root}` from the workflow context. This is set during workflow initialization from the COOKIE project configuration.
Join: `{project-root}` + `/` + relative_path. Normalize the resulting path (resolve `..`, remove double slashes).
Return the absolute path.

resolver.description: Paths containing `{variable}` placeholders are resolved by substituting each placeholder with its value from the workflow context. All placeholders must resolve or the path is invalid.

known_variables.variable: Absolute path to the COOKIE project root directory.
Absolute path to the current episode directory.
Identifier for the current episode (e.g., ep01, ep02).
Identifier for the current scene within an episode.
Identifier for the current character being processed.
Unique ID of the running workflow instance.
Path to the current episode's SSD directory.

procedure.step: Scan the path string for all `{variable}` patterns.

For each placeholder found: a) Look up the variable name in the workflow context. b) If found, substitute the placeholder with the resolved value. c) If NOT found, trigger `on_error.unresolved_variable` and halt.
After all substitutions, normalize the path. Apply ref_prefix resolution if the result starts with `ref:`.
Return the fully resolved absolute path.

resolver.description: Absolute paths without `ref:` prefix or `{variable}` placeholders. Used as-is after path normalization.

procedure.step: Verify the path is absolute (starts with `/`).
Normalize the path (resolve `..`, remove double slashes).
Return the normalized absolute path.

strategy_selection.description: When an input declaration does not explicitly specify a loading strategy, the engine auto-selects the best strategy based on the input characteristics.

rule.condition: Input path resolves to a directory (not a file).
Input path resolves to a single file ≤ 512KB.
Input path resolves to a single file > 512KB.
rule.action: Use SELECTIVE_LOAD strategy with auto-generated selectors based on step requirements.
Log a warning. Attempt FULL_LOAD as fallback. If it fails, trigger on_error.validation_failure.

error_handling.description: When a resolved path does not point to an existing file or directory, the protocol triggers the appropriate error handler from error-recovery.xml.

on_error.description: Triggered when a resolved file path does not exist on disk.

procedure.step: Log the missing file path and the input declaration that referenced it.
Check if the input is marked as `optional: true` in the workflow step. If optional, log a warning and continue with a null value in context.

If the input is required, delegate to the `missing_file` handler defined in error-recovery.xml: - Present the missing path to the user. - Ask for the correct path or permission to skip. - If user provides a new path, re-run path resolution and validation. - If user chooses to skip, mark the input as skipped in context and let the step decide how to handle the missing data.

on_error.description: Triggered when a `{variable}` placeholder cannot be resolved from the workflow context during path resolution.

procedure.step: Immediately halt path resolution for this input.

Delegate to the `unresolved_variable` handler in error-recovery.xml. The workflow will halt with a clear error message listing: - The unresolved variable name. - The full path pattern that contains it. - The workflow step that declared the input. - Suggestions for where the variable should have been set.

context_output.description: After discovery and loading, all resolved inputs are stored in the workflow context under a structured namespace for the current step.

entry.field: The loaded content (parsed object, string, or collection).
The fully resolved absolute path to the source file.
The loading strategy that was used (FULL_LOAD, SELECTIVE_LOAD, INDEX_GUIDED).
The selector expression used, if any (null for FULL_LOAD).
ISO 8601 timestamp of when the content was loaded.
Size of the source file in bytes.
SHA-256 hash of the loaded content for cache validation.
